content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 7, 1998

Chris P. Mathys
Member, Fresno City Council &
County Central Committee
Fresno City Hall
2600 Fresno Street
Fresno, California 92721

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-98-226

Dear Mr. Mathys:

This is in response to your request for informal assistance regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please bear in mind that nothing in this letter
should be construed as evaluation of any conduct which may have already taken place. Because
you are requesting advice regarding a general application of the law, your letter dated August 21,
1998, is considered a request for informal assistance.²

QUESTION/ANSWER

You have asked if you are required to set up a separate committee and campaign bank
account for the purpose of raising funds for officeholder related expenses as a member of a
county central committee? The answer is yes.

___________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329, subd. (c)(3).) We note that you are requesting confirmation of
telephone advice. While informal assistance may be requested or rendered orally or in writing, assistance will only
be rendered concerning prospective conduct. (Regulation 18329, subd. (c)(4).) Presumably, your request for
written advice involves only prospective conduct.

File No. I-98-226
Page 2

DISCUSSION

You are an incumbent city council member and still have a campaign committee in
existence for this office.

In June 1998 you were elected to the county central committee. Your letter states a Form
501, Candidate Intention and a Form 470 were filed in connection with the June 1998 election.
You have requested advice concerning the use of your current city council committee and
campaign account for raising funds to assist you with your duties as a member of the central
committee. Campaign funds shall only be spent for expenses associated with the specific
elective office. (Section 85201, Reg. 18524.) Therefore, a separate committee and a separate
campaign bank account would have to be established. (Sections 84101 and 85201.)

Because you have more than one controlled committee, you and your controlled
committees must file statements each time a committee statement is due. In addition, because
your committees have been established for different offices in different jurisdictions, both
committees must file statements with the filing officer in each of the jurisdictions in which you
hold office. (Section 84215(d) and (e), Page 16, 1995/96 Information Manual A.)

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at 916/322-5660.

Sincerely

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant
